Dear Ms. Hoff:

Our corporate legal assistant, Mrs. Ginger Mutoza, spoke with
you last week regarding WACMC. Our question is whether the direc-
tors of WACMC need to file conflict of interests statements.

Some of the directors will be designated employees of various
local agencies located within Monterey County.
whether these directors of WACMC, by their employment with local
agencies are subject to the Political Reform Act and need to file

Statements of Economic Interest (Form 730).

Our question is

Pursuant to your request, attached is a copy of the Articles
of Incorporation and pertinent pages of the draft bylaws for WACMC

Please feel free to contact either Mrs.

Ginger Mutoza, our cor-

porate legal assistant, or me if you have any questions.

Your advise on the matter will be appreciated.

ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION

OF MAR 19 199}
WATER AWARENESS COMMITTEE OF MONTEREY COUNTY
MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State
I

The name of this Corporation is WATER AWARENESS COMMITTEE oF
MONTEREY COUNTY.

II

A. This Corporation is a nonprofit public benefit
corporation and is not organized for the private gain of any
person. It is organized under the Nonprofit Public Benefit
Corporation Law for charitable purposes.

B. The specific purpose of this Corporation is to bring
together and coordinate representatives of the diverse interests
in the community and water industry from the different geographic
areas, and from the public and private sector, for the purpose of
citizens’ education on water and its influences on the community.

Ci Additional purposes of this corporation are:

is To combine the resources, efforts, and expertise
available in the community and water industry to develop
citizens’ education campaigns and programs, and serve as the
vehicle to raise the level of awareness in the community of water
resources and water resource-related issues.

ii. To create a stable mechanism and official entity
for coordinating the diverse interests within Monterey County
behind a goal of promoting knowledge and awareness of water
resource issues and topics.

IIft

The name and address in this State of the Corporation's

initial agent for service of process is:

Ann St. Pierre
5 Harris Court, Bldg. D,
Monterey, California 93940

A. This corporation is organized and operated exclusively
for charitable purposes within the meaning of section 501(c)(3)
of the Internal Revenue Code.

B. No substantial part of the activities of this
Corporation shall consist of carrying on propaganda, or otherwise
attempting to influence legislation, and the Corporation shall
not participate or intervene in any political campaign (including
the publishing or distribution of statements) on behalf of any
candidate for public office.

Vv

The name of the existing unincorporated association, now
incorporated by the filing of these Articles is WATER AWARENESS
COMMITTEE OF MONTEREY COUNTY.

VI

The property of this Corporation is irrevocably dedicated to
charitable purposes and no part of the net income or assets of
this Corporation shall ever inure to the benefit of any director,
officer or member thereof or to the benefit of any private
person. Upon the dissolution or winding up of the Corporation,
its assets remaining after payment, or provision for payment, of
all debts and liabilities of this Corporation shall be
distributed to a nonprofit fund, foundation or corporation which
is organized and operated exclusively for citizen education in
water conservation purposes and which has established its tax

exempt status under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue

Code.

association referred to in the Articles of Incorporation to which
this declaration is attached, and that said association has duly

BYLAWS OF
WATER AWARENESS COMMITTEE OF MONTEREY COUNTY
A California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation

ARTICLE 1. OFFICES
eee i PRINCIPAL OFFICE.

The principal office of the Corporation for the transac-
tion of its business is located at 5 Harris Court, Building D,
Monterey, California 93940.

1.2 GE OF ESS.

The county of the Corporation’s principal office can be

changed only by amendment of these Bylaws and not otherwise.
d-0'3 OTHER OFFICES.

The Corporation may also have offices at such other
places, within or without the State of California, where it is
qualified to do business, as its business May require and as the
Board of Directors may, from time to time, designate.

ARTICLE 2. PURPOSES
2s1 OBJECTIVES AND PURPOSES.

This Corporation is organized under the Nonprofit Public
Benefit Corporation Law for charitable and educational purposes,
The specific purposes for which this Corporation is organized are:

2:2..8 To educate the people of Monterey County about
water and its importance to Monterey County.

Zalad To raise the level of public awareness about
water resources and water resource-relatea issues.

2.1.3 To encourage wise stewardship of Monterey
County’s water resources,

2.1.4 To bring together and coordinate the diverse |
resources and expertise available from the private and public sec-
tors throughout Monterey County, to accomplish the Corporation’s
goals of water resource education, awareness and stewardship.

ARTICLE 3. MEMBERS
3.1 MEMBERS.

The Corporation shall have no members, as defined by
Corporations Code section 5056(a). The Corporation’s Board of
Directors shall govern the. Corporation in all respects, and shall
exercise all of the voting rights which would otherwise be exer~
cised by members pursuant to section 5056. The Corporation may
have persons. designated or denominated as "members," but such
designation or denomination shall not confer on such persons any
rights not given Specifically by these bylaws.

ARTICLE 4, ASSOCIATES
4.1 ASSOCIATES.

The Corporation shall have a class of persons and
organizations designated "associates." Associates shall
contribute dues of at least $1,000.00 annually, or contribute
materials, labor or services of equivalent value as determined by
the Board.

4.2 ADMISSION AS ASSOCIATE.

Any person qualified and eligible for membership under
Section 4.1 of these Bylaws shall be admitted as an associate only
on the approval of the Board of Directors of an application sub-
mitted by such person in such form and in such manner as shall be
prescribed by the Board of Directors and on the payment of the sums
and making the commitments specified in Section 4.4 of these
Bylaws.

4.3 APPLICATION FEE.

There shall be no fee for making application for associ-
ate status in the Corporation.

4.4 COMMITMENTS REQUIRED FROM ASSOCIATES. ;

4.4.1 The annual dues and contributions of associates
Shall be in such amounts as shall be determined by resolution of
the Board of Directors. Dues shall be payable for the first year
on admission to associate status and annually thereafter at such
time as may be fixed by the Board of Directors. An associate, on
learning of the amount of dues determined by the Board of

Directors, may ‘avoid liability for the dues by promptly resigning
from associate status, except where the associate is, by contract
or otherwise, liable for the dues.

4.5 ASSESSMENTS.

-.. . .Associate status.shall.be.nonassessable. — Fehon

4.6 NUMBER OF ASSOCIATES.

-There shall be no limit on the number of associates the
Corporation may. admit. .

‘4.7 ASSOCIATE BOOK.

The Corporation shall keep in written form an associate
book containing the name, address and class of each associate. The
book shall contain the fact of termination and the Gate on which
such associate status ceased. Such book shall be kept at the prin-
cipal office of the Corporation and shall be subject to inspection
by the Directors.

- 4.8 CERTIFICATES OF ASSOCIATE STATUS.

The Corporation shall not issue associate status certi-
ficates, however, the Corporation reserves the right to issue iden-
tity cards or similar devices to associates which serve to identify
associates qualifying to use the facilities or services of the
Corporation.

4.9 NONLIABILITY OF ASSOCIATES.

An associate of the Corporation shall not solely because
of such associate status be personally liable for the debts, obli-
gations, or liabilities of the Corporation.

4.10 MTRANSFERABILITY OF ASSOCIATE STATUS.

Neither the associate status in the Corporation nor any
rights in the associate status may be transferred for value or
otherwise.

4.11 QERMINATION OF ASSOCIATE STATUS.

4.11.1 Causes.

An associate shall be automatically terminated
on the occurrence of any of the following events:

4.11.1.1 The voluntary resignation of an
associate on reasonable notice to the corporation.

o.

4.11.2.4 Any action challenging an associate’s
termination including a claim alleging defective notice, must be
commenced within one year after the date of the termination.

4.11.3 Effect of Termination.

All rights. ofan associate in the Corporation.
and in its property shall cease on termination of such associate’s
good standing. Termination. shall not relieve the associate from:
any obligation for charges incurred, .services and benefits actually
rendered, dues, assessments, or fees, or arising from contract or
otherwise. The Corporation shall retain the right to enforce any
such obligation or obtain damages for its breach.

ARTICLE 5. MEETINGS OF ASSOCIATES
5.1 PLACE.

Meetings of some or all of the associates may be held at
any time and at any location within the state of California as may
be designated from time to time by resolution of the Board of
Directors. The Board may give such notice of any such meetings in
such manner as the Board determines is appropriate.

52 VOTING OF ASSOCIATES.
5.2.1 Entitlement.

The Board may seek the advice of some or ali of
the associates on any matter, and may request an advisory vote on
any matter, using such means and procedures as the Board may con-
sider appropriate.

5.3 QUORUM.

The Board shall determine the quorum requirements, if
any, of any meeting of Associates. If the Board sets a quorum for
any meeting, it shall also determine the consequences of not having
a quorum present...

ARTICLE 6. DIRECTORS
6.1 NUMBER.

The Corporation shall have no less than 5, and no more
than 11 directors, the exact number to be set annually by the
directors. The initial number of directors shall be set at 9.

6.2 POWERS.

Subject to the provisions of the California Nonprofit
Public Benefit Corporation Law and any limitations in the Articles
of Incorporation and Bylaws, the activities and affairs of this
Corporation shall be conducte@ and all. corporate powers shall be
exercised by or under the direction of the Board of Directors.

6.3... DUTIES. -

It shall be the duty of. the Directors to:

S134 Perform any and all duties imposed on them col-
lectively or individually by law, by the Articles of Incorporation
of this Corporation, or by these Bylaws.

6.3.2 Select successor directors as provided in these
Bylaws.

6.3.3 Appoint and remove, employ and discharge, and,
except as otherwise provided in these Bylaws, prescribe the duties
and fix the compensation, if any, of all officers, agents and
employees of the Corporation.

6.3.4 Supervise all officers, agents and employees of
the Corporation to assure that their duties are performed properly.

6.3.5 Meet at such times and places as required by
these Bylaws.

6.36 Register their addresses with the Secretary of
the Corporation, and notices of meetings mailed or telegraphed to
them at such addresses shall be valid notices thereof.

6.4 ELECTION OF DIRECTORS.

644.4 Of the nine (9) initial Directors three (3) Dir-
ectors shall serve for one (1) year, three (3) shall serve for two
(2) years, and three (3) shall serve for three (3) years. No
Director shall serve more than two (2) consecutive three (3) year.
terms. .

6.4.2 Upon the expiration of a Director’s term, new
Directors shall be elected by the Directors at the annual meeting,
to serve a term of three (3) years, or until the Director’s
successor is selected. In any event, the election of successor
Directors shall be conducted before January 1 of each year.
Directors shall take office upon election.


6.4.3 The President shall, in advance of the election
meeting, appoint a nominating committee comprised of two (2) Board
members, to nominate a slate of candidates equal in numbers to
those Directors whose term will expire. The President shall aesig-
nate.an individual.to be Chairman of this committee. Additional
candidates may be nominated by other Directors at the annual
meeting. _

6.5 COMPENSATION...

Directors shall serve without compensation, except that they
shall be allowed and paid their actual and necessary expenses in-
curred in activities as authorized by the Board. In addition, they
shall be allowed a reasonable advancement or reimbursement for
expenses incurred in this Article. Directors may not be
compensated for rendering services to the Corporation in any
Capacity other than Director unless such other compensation is
reasonable and is allowable under the provisions of Section 6.6 of
this Article.

6.6 RESTRICTION REGARDING INTERESTED DIRECTORS.

Notwithstanding any other provision of these Bylaws, not
more than forty-nine percent (49%) of the persons serving on the
Board may be interested persons. For purposes of this Section,
“interested persons" means either:

6.6.1 Any person currently being compensated by the
Corporation for services rendered it within the previous twelve
(12) months, whether as a full- or part-time officer or other
employee, independent contractor, or otherwise, excluding any rea-
sonable compensation paid to a Director as Director; or

6.6.2 Any brother, sister, ancestor, descendant,
Spouse, brother-in~law, sister-in-law, son-in-law, daughter-in-law,
mother-in-law, or father-in-law of any such person,

6.7 PLACE OF MEETINGS.

Meetings shall be held at the principal office of the
Corporation unless otherwise provided by the Board or at such place

6.20 INSURANCE FOR CORPORATE AGENTS.

The Board of Directors may adopt a resolution authoriz-
ing the purchase and maintenance of insurance on behalf of any
agent of the Corporation (including a Director, officer, employee
or other-agent.of the.Corporation) . against..any liability other than
for violating provisions of law relating to self-dealing (section
5233 of the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law)
asserted against or incurred by the agent.in such capacity or aris-
ing out of the agent’ status as such, whether or not the Corpora-
tion would have the power to indemnify the agent against such lia-
bility under the provisions of section 5238 of the California
Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law. The Directors shall
diligently attempt to obtain insurance sufficient to Satisfy the
requirements of section 5239 of the California Nonprofit Public
Benefit Corporation Law.

| ARTICLE 7. OFFICERS
7.1 NUMBER OF OFFICERS.

The officers of this Corporation shall be a Chairperson
of the Board, a President, a Secretary and a chief financial offi-
cer who shall be designated the Treasurer. The Corporation may
also have, as determined by the Board of Directors, one or more
Vice-Presidents, Assistant Secretaries, Assistant Treasurers, or
other officers. Any number of offices may be held by the same
person except that neither the Secretary nor the Treasurer may
serve as the President or Chairperson of the Board.

V2 UALIFICATIO ELECTION, AND OF 0 CE.

“Any person may serve as officer of this Corporation,
Officers shall be elected by the Board of Directors, at any time,
and each officer shall hold office until he or she resigns or is .
removed or is otherwise disqualified to serve, or until his or her
successor shall be elected and qualified, whichever occurs first.

7.2 SUBORDINATE OFFICERS.

The Board of Directors may appoint such other officers

or agents as it may deem desirable, and such officers shall serve

- 13 -

a)
ina)
m


such terms, have such authority, and perform such duties as may be
prescribed from time to time by the Board of Directors.
7.4 OVAL AND RESIGNATION.

Any officer may be removed, either with or without
cause,-.by. the .Board..of.Directors, at.any time. Any. officer. may ....
resign at any time by. giving written notice to the Board of Direc-
tors or to the President or Secretary of the Corporation. Any such
resignation. shall take effect at the date of the receipt of such
notice or at any later: date specified therein, and, unless other-
wise specified therein, the acceptance of such resignation shall
not be necessary to make it effective. The above provisions of
this Section shall be superseded by any conflicting terms of a
contract which has been approved or ratified by the Board of Direc-
tors relating to the employment of any officer of the Corporation.

Ve 5 VACANCIES.

Any vacancy caused by the death, resignation, removal,
disqualification, or otherwise, of any officer shall be filled by.
the Board of Directors. In the event of a vacancy in any office
other than that of President,. such vacancy may be filled temporar-
ily by appointment by the President until such time as the Board
shall fi11 the vacancy. Vacancies occurring in offices of officers
appointed at the discretion of the Board May Or may not be filled
as the Board shall determine.

7.6 ES OF PR ENT,

The President shall be the chief executive officer of
the Corporation and shall, subject to the control of the Board of
Directors, supervise and control the affairs of the Corporation and
the activities of the officers. He or she shall perform all duties
incident to his or her office and such other duties as may be
required by law, by the Articles of Incorporation of this Corpora-
tion, or by these Bylaws, or which may be prescribed from time to
time by the Board of Directors. Unless another person is specifi-
cally appointed as Chairman of the Board of Directors, he or she
shall preside at all meetings of the Board of Directors. If appli-
cable, the President shall preside at all meetings of the

associates. Ekcept as otherwise expressly provided by law, by the
Articles of Incorporation, or by these Bylaws, he or she shall, in
the name of the Corporation, execute such deeds, mortgages, bonds,
contracts, checks, or other instruments which may from time to time
be authorized by. the Board of. Directors,

7.7 DUTIES OF VICE-PRESIDENT.

In the absence of the President, or in the event of his
or her inability or refusal to act, the Vice-President shall per-
form all the duties of the President, and when so acting shall have
all the powers of, and be subject to all the restrictions on, the
President. The Vice-President shall have other powers and perform
such other duties as may be prescribed by law, by the Articles of
Incorporation, or by these Bylaws, or as may be prescribed by the
Board of Directors.

7.8 D ES SEC Ye

The Secretary: shall:

- Certify and keep at the principal office of the Corpo-
ration the original, or a copy, of these Bylaws as amended or
otherwise altered to date. .

- Keep at the principal office of the Corporation or at
such other place as the Board may determine, a book of minutes of
all meetings of the Directors, and, if applicable, meetings of
committees of Directors and of associates, recording therein the
time and place of holding, whether regular or special, how called,
how notice thereof was given, the names of those present or repre-
sented at the meeting, and the proceedings thereof.

- See that all: notices are duly given in accordance with
the provisions of these Bylaws or as required by law.

- Be custodian of the records and of the seal of the
Corporation and see that the seal is affixed to all duly executed
documents, the execution of which on behalf of the Corporation
under its seal is authorized by law or by these Bylaws.

: ~ Keep at the principal office of the Corporation a
associate status book containing the name and address of each and
every associates, and, in the case where any associate status has

~ Exhibit at all reasonable times to any Director of the
Corporation, or to, ,his_or her agent.or attorney, on request there-
for, the Bylaws, the associate status book, and the minutes of the
proceedings of the Directors of the Corporation.

- In general, perform all duties incident to the office
of —— and such other duties as may be required by law, by-
the Articles of Incorporation of this Corporation, or by these
Bylaws, or which may be assigned to him or her from time to time by
the Board of Directors. He or she shall execute such deeds, mort-
gages, bonds, contracts, checks, or other instruments which from
time to time may be authorized by the Board of Directors.

7.9 DUTIES OF TREASURER.

Subject to the provisions of these Bylaws relating to
the "Execution of Instruments, Deposits and Funds," the Treasurer
shall:

7 Have charge and custody of, and be responsible for,
all funds and securities of the Corporation, and deposit all such
funds in the name of the Corporation in such banks, trust com-
panies, or other depositories as shall be selected by the Board of
Directors.

- Receive, and give receipt for, monies due and payable
to the Corporation from any source whatsoever.

- Disburse or cause to be disbursed the funds of the
Corporation as may be directed by the Board of Directors, taking
proper vouchers for such disbursements.

- Keep and maintain adequate and correct accounts of the
Corporation’s properties and business transactions, including
accounts of its assets, liabilities, receipts, disbursements, gains
and losses.

- Exhibit at all reasonable times the books of account
and financial records to any Director of the Corporation, or to his
or her agent or attorney, on request therefor.

- Render to the President and Directors, whenever oa
requested, an account of any or all of his or her transactions as
{Treasurer and of the financial condition of the Corporation.

~ Prepare, or. cause to be prepared, and certify, or
caused..to.be certified, the financial. statements to be included. in
any required reports. -

- In general, perform all duties incident to the office
of Treasurer and such other duties. as. may-be required by law, by
the Articles of Incorporation of the Corporation, or by these By-
laws, or which may be assigned to him or her from time to time by
the Board of Directors.

7.10 COMPENSATION.

The corporation’s officers shall receive no compensation
but may be reimbursed for out of pocket expenses incurred in
activities as authorized by the Board.

ARTICLE 8. COMMITTEES
Sigd: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE.

The Board of Directors may, by a majority vote of Direc-
tors then in office, designate two (2) or more of its Directors
(who may also be serving as officers of this Corporation) to con-
stitute an Executive Committee and delegate to such Committee any
of the powers and authority of the Board in the management of the
business and affairs of the Corporation, except with respect to:

8.1.1. The approval of any action which, under law or
the provisions of these Bylaws, requires the approval of the
members or of a majority of all of the members.

a The filling of vacancies on the Board or on any
committee which has the authority of the Board. .

8.1.3 The fixing of compensation of the Directors for
serving on the Board or on any committee.

8.1.4 The amendment or repeal of Bylaws or the adop-
tion of new Bylaws.

8.1.5 The amendment or repeal of any resolution of the
Board which by its express terms is not so amendable or repealable.

8.1.6 Create any other committees of the Board or
appoint the members of committees of the Board;

8.1.7 ‘Expend corporate funds to Support a nominee for

8.1.8 - The approval of any transaction to which this
Corporation is a.party and in which one or more of the Directors
has a.material. financial interest, except as expressly. provided in
section 5233(d) (3) of the-California Nonprofit Public Benefit
Corporation Law.

The Board may by a majority vote at any time revoke or
modify any or all of the authority so delegated, increase or
decrease but not below two (2) the number of its committee members,
and fill vacancies therein from the Board. The Committee shall
keep regular minutes of its proceedings, cause them to be filed
with the corporate records, and report the same to the Board from
time to time as the Board may require.

8.2 OTHER COMMITTEES.

The Corporation shall have such other committees as may
from time to time be designated by resolution of the Board of
Directors. Such other committees may constitute of persons who are
not also associates of the Board. These additional committees
Shall act in an advisory capacity only to the Board and shall be
Clearly titled as "advisory" committees.

8.3 MEETINGS AND ACTION OF co EES.

Meetings and action of committees shall be governed by,
noticed, held and taken in accordance with the provisions of these
Bylaws concerning meetings of the Board of Directors, with such
changes in the context of. such Bylaws provisions as are necessary.
to substitute the committee and its associates for the Board of
Directors and its associates, except that the time for regular
meetings of committees may be fixed by resolution of the Board of
Directors or by the committee. The time for special meetings of

embedding.qa_text: April 8, 1991 VIA FAX Ms. Cheryl Hoff Fair Political Practices Committee Re: Water Awareness Committee of Monterey County (WACMC) A California Non-Profit Public Benefit Corporation Dear Ms. Hoff: Our corporate legal assistant, Mrs. Ginger Mutoza, spoke with you last week regarding WACMC. Our question is whether the directors of WACMC need to file conflict of interests statements. Some of the directors will be designated employees of various local agencies located within Monterey County. Our question is whether these directors of WACMC, by their employment with local agencies are subject to the Political Reform Act and need to file Statements of Economic Interest (Form 730). Pursuant to your request, attached is a copy of the Articles of Incorporation and pertinent pages of the draft bylaws for WACMC. Please feel free to contact either Mrs. Ginger Mutoza, our corporate legal assistant, or me if you have any questions. Your advise on the matter will be appreciated. Very truly yours, Lloyd W. Lowrey, Jr. NOLAND, HAMERLY, ETIENNE & HOSS A Professional Corporation ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION OF WATER AWARENESS COMMITTEE OF MONTEREY COUNTY I The name of this Corporation is WATER AWARENESS COMMITTEE OF MONTEREY COUNTY. II A. This Corporation is a nonprofit public benefit corporation and is not organized for the private gain of any person. It is organized under the Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law for charitable purposes. B. The specific purpose of this Corporation is to bring together and coordinate representatives of the diverse interests in the community and water industry from the different geographic areas, and from the public and private sector, for the purpose of citizens' education on water and its influences on the community. C. Additional purposes of this corporation are: i. To combine the resources, efforts, and expertise available in the community and water industry to develop citizens' education campaigns and programs, and serve as the vehicle to raise the level of awareness in the community of water resources and water resource-related issues. ii. To create a stable mechanism and official entity for coordinating the diverse interests within Monterey County behind a goal of promoting knowledge and awareness of water resource issues and topics. III The name and address in this State of the Corporation's initial agent for service of process is: Ann St. Pierre 5 Harris Court, Bldg. D, Monterey, California 93940 A. This corporation is organized and operated exclusively for charitable purposes within the meaning of section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code. B. No substantial part of the activities of this Corporation shall consist of carrying on propaganda, or otherwise attempting to influence legislation, and the Corporation shall not participate or intervene in any political campaign (including the publishing or distribution of statements) on behalf of any candidate for public office. V The name of the existing unincorporated association, now incorporated by the filing of these Articles is WATER AWARENESS COMMITTEE OF MONTEREY COUNTY. VI The property of this Corporation is irrevocably dedicated to charitable purposes and no part of the net income
embedding.first_500_words: April 8, 1991 VIA FAX Ms. Cheryl Hoff Fair Political Practices Committee Re: Water Awareness Committee of Monterey County (WACMC) A California Non-Profit Public Benefit Corporation Dear Ms. Hoff: Our corporate legal assistant, Mrs. Ginger Mutoza, spoke with you last week regarding WACMC. Our question is whether the directors of WACMC need to file conflict of interests statements. Some of the directors will be designated employees of various local agencies located within Monterey County. Our question is whether these directors of WACMC, by their employment with local agencies are subject to the Political Reform Act and need to file Statements of Economic Interest (Form 730). Pursuant to your request, attached is a copy of the Articles of Incorporation and pertinent pages of the draft bylaws for WACMC. Please feel free to contact either Mrs. Ginger Mutoza, our corporate legal assistant, or me if you have any questions. Your advise on the matter will be appreciated. Very truly yours, Lloyd W.
